page: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. This configuration provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 50 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES cryptography, BMI2, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with the instance, providing 102 GB of capacity. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in the base configuration, and there are no hardware GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the D4_v3 demonstrates entry-level performance characteristics. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10%, while small-block cached reads reach 173.55 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression represents a relative bright spot, performing in the middle 50% tier at 24.35 GB/sec, whereas bzip3 decompression and database operations remain in the lower percentiles.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4_v3 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and a standard 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio without the premium of newer-generation hardware. Due to its modest processing and memory throughput, this server is best suited for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, development and testing environments, or low-throughput background tasks rather than resource-intensive databases or high-performance computing.
description: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor offering 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores with hyper-threading) clocked at 2.3 GHz, alongside 16.0 GB of system memory and 102 GB of local storage. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench CPU scores falling in the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth largely constrained to the bottom 10% to 25% tiers. However, it achieves average performance in single-threaded gzip compression. Lacking GPU acceleration and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512, the D4_v3 represents a budget-friendly option for workloads with modest resource demands. It is best suited for light web serving, basic development or testing environments, and low-intensity background processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a Dv3-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, offering entry-level general-purpose performance for basic workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, designed for entry-level workloads.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, balanced memory allocation, and local storage for modest utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 102 GB of local storage
Delivers entry-level CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Achieves average throughput in single-threaded gzip compression
Suitable for light web serving, development, and testing environments
